title: What pi-context is — the framework's generative model (organizing principles, audited)
question: What does the pi-context framework provide as fixed scaffolding versus config-declared vocabulary, and how does .context instantiate that general model — as a usage / how-to / what-is-pi-context reference grounded against current code and substrate.
method: code-inspection + live-substrate-query audit: an agent-written explainer verified claim-by-claim against packages/pi-context/src and the active .context substrate via the pi-context CLI (read-config / read-schema / read-block / find-references / context-validate).
findings_summary: pi-context is a declarative project operating system over typed JSON blocks: the framework is fixed scaffolding, the vocabulary is config-declared. A substrate declares block_kinds (schema-validated JSON files; content-addressed identity via oid/content_hash; schema_version + migrations.json forward-migration), relation_types (closure-table edges {parent,child,relation_type,ordinal} in relations.json — the ONLY linkage, no FK-as-field, each typed with source_kinds/target_kinds), invariants (cross-block constraints enforced by context-validate), status_buckets (status->lifecycle bucket; an OVERRIDE layer over hardcoded STATUS_VOCABULARY_DEFAULTS — bucket vocabulary complete/in_progress/blocked/todo/unknown, no 'open' bucket), lenses (derived_from_field or hand-curated views), and conventions (block-kind prose rules with enforcement+severity, some checked by requires-edge invariants). All of it is surfaced through one op-registry reflected by the pi-context CLI (pi-context <op>) and the in-pi tool surface. .context instantiates this as a software-project model: 17 block kinds (decisions, framework-gaps, tasks, verification, features, research, conventions, ...), 33 relation_types, and an arc model where a feature binds gaps decomposed into tasks — derivable as feature -> task_addresses_feature -> tasks -> task_addresses_gap -> gaps — with governance via conventions plus FEAT-007's convention-articulation invariants. A custom-vocabulary substrate (.context-jit-spec-v2: axioms/concepts/dispatch-modes) declares a different ontology over the same scaffolding. Two coupling points remain hardcoded (context-current-state, context-status; FGAP-017/018) pending FEAT-004's state-derivation registry; append-block-item lacks --dry-run (FGAP-024, open). What any substrate gets for free, without writing code: typed+validated state, stable content-addressed identity, typed cross-item relationships, lifecycle aggregation by bucket, integrity + governance validation, versioned schema migration, catalog distribution (pi-context update merges catalog vocabulary additively), content-addressed lineage, and a reflecting CLI surfacing every registered op. This entry curates the full per-section explainer in the findings_document; an embedded audit verified it against code + substrate — 22 claims Verified, 3 Incorrect + 2 Stale corrected in-body (the 'open' bucket, the FGAP-017/018 status-bucket misattribution, the universal --dry-run claim, the defaults-scope, and the roadmap.json reference).
stale_conditions: a block_kind / relation_type / lens / invariant / status_bucket is added or removed from .context config (the 17/33/2 counts and registry claims drift)
the op-registry surface changes (op added/removed/renamed — the CLI-reflection + op-name claims drift)
FEAT-004 lands (the context-current-state/context-status hardcoded-coupling claims in section 7 become false)
FGAP-024 closes (the 'append-block-item lacks --dry-run' claim becomes false)
STATUS_VOCABULARY_DEFAULTS changes (the bucket-mapping claims in section 3 drift)
